campaign: A4 — Cost/concurrency budget + revert path + reclaim (trust-first guardrails)

phase_outcomes.P4: Tests/seal + metrics DATA surface + campaign close (FINAL A4 phase). The metrics surface A5 visualizes: an ADDITIVE auto_implement sub-block on the existing on-read GET /projects/{id}/escalations/metrics — escalation-LINKED-MR-derived (the rate data was already queryable from merge_requests: escalationId IS NOT NULL = the auto-implement universe, null on every normal worker/MCP MR; status=landed/rejected; revertOf IS NOT NULL = reverts). computeAutoImplement(projectId): ONE grouped fetch over merge_requests scoped to (projectId AND escalationId IS NOT NULL) → {autoImplementedEscalations (COUNT DISTINCT escalationId), landed, rejected, reverts, landRate=landed/(landed+rejected), rejectRate, revertRate=reverts/landed} (rates null on zero-denominator). spendPerArc OMITTED — no token source (P1 deferred token accounting; runners declare tokensConsumed but never report back; parked). Wired into EscalationMetricsBundle + the route zod schema (after by_kind) + metricsToResponse camel→snake. INERT (zeros, null rates) when no linked MRs ⇒ byte-identical pre-A4. NO new migration (rates derived), NO new responder code, NO new engine. Seal also closed a latent P2 gap: the merge-group member-view pinned-key seals (merge-group.test + merge-groups route test) had not been updated for P2's revertOf column → added 'revertOf' to both PINNED_KEYS (the view spreads the full row, established idiom). Docs: CLAUDE.md A4 paragraph (after A3) + responder-ref README Guardrails (Campaign A4) section + the 3 P1/P3 env-var rows (MAX_CONCURRENT_ARCS=100 / MAX_ARC_DURATION_SEC=604800 / STALL_TIMEOUT_SEC=86400) the README never got. FULL-CAMPAIGN SEAL all green: server 1726 (+10), responder 186, shared 514, integrator 329 (+1 skip). openapi:export + web generate:api propagated the new field; build 7/7, typecheck 9/9, lint 0 errors. A1-A3/C1-C4/answer-mode byte-identical; ships enabled=false. CAMPAIGN A4 COMPLETE.
phase_outcomes.P3: Per-MR stall reclaim. Gap analysis: gap(a) slot-leak ALREADY CLOSED (in-memory implementSlots resets on restart). REAL residual = a per-MR stall timeout covering BOTH (i) phase MRs (advanceArc's step-1 re-park `return` at ~1374 fired EVERY cycle on a wedged queued/integrating MR, and the P1 whole-arc duration cap at ~1471 sat AFTER it → UNREACHABLE → a single stuck MR was unbounded: a confirmed latent P1 dead-cap bug) and (ii) bounded pendingLand MRs (the reclaim sweep skipped them UNCONDITIONALLY/timeout-less → stuck acknowledged forever). FIX: one shared isMrStalled(mr,now,stallTimeoutSec) (status∈{queued,integrating} && now - submitClockMs(mr) > stallTimeoutSec*1000 + grace; grace=max(120s,0.25×budget) per 7.6.1) evaluated AT each wait point — submitClockMs parses enqueuedAt??createdAt failing to +INFINITY (absent⇒never stalls, mirrors updatedAtMs NOT createdAtMs's 0). Site 1 (advanceArc re-park): stalled phase MR → escalate root arc_partial-shaped (landed phases preserved) before re-park. Site 2 (bounded pendingLand skip, surgical — A3-P3 arc routing + poison-cap path untouched): probe listMergeRequests(escalationId) → reconcile-first (landed→skip, A2 owns) → stalled→escalateToHuman (branch preserved) → within-window→skip. Server-derived ⇒ restart-resilient; reconcile-before-escalate (no work discarded); non-fatal probe. config: autoImplement.budget.stallTimeoutSec=86400 (24h, finer than the 7-day arc cap) + env. Generous default ⇒ A1-A4P2 byte-identical; markers only on the auto_implement path ⇒ answer-mode untouched. NO server/shared/openapi change (MR view already carries enqueuedAt/createdAt). 186 responder tests (+8). build/typecheck/lint green. f2c784b.
phase_outcomes.P2: Fast revert path. A one-command revert of a landed_sha rides the SAME verify-gated merge train (main never breaks reverting — verify runs against the rebased tree before FF). Server has NO git + the responder tail is escalation-welded, so adjudicated: INTEGRATOR-produces. REST POST /projects/{id}/merge-requests/revert {landedSha,resource?,reason?,escalationId?} (any-authed, mirror 7.4 break-glass) → submitRevert inserts a branchless queued MR {taskId:null, branch:null, revertOf:landedSha, escalationId} (copies an existing landed MR's verifyCmd for the same sha, else project default). The integrator materializes the branch at the EXISTING null-branch-safe pickup seam (loop.ts: after baseSha, the `ref=branch??commitSha; if(!ref)reject` point; batch.ts: after reset, before computeSpeculativeBase, reset-back-to-clean-main) PRESENCE-GUARDED on revertOf!=null&&branch==null: gitOps.revert(sha) (git revert --no-edit, conflict→--abort→reject; +createBranch helper) → push pm/revert-<sha> → set branch → everything downstream byte-identical. DETERMINISTIC (no LLM → no injection surface). revertOf column (migration 0033_bumpy_apocalypse, PLAIN nullable text NOT an FK — a landed sha possibly landed out-of-band) threaded through row/view/list-filter + @pm/shared zod-3 + route zod-4 mirror + openapi/web regen; migration-journal.test LAST_REVERT/LAST_COLUMN_CHECK bumped. Queryable audit chain (?revertOf=sha) + timeline renders it; reuses the A2 escalation post-back (land→resolve, reject→needs_human). Single-sha v1 (arc-level=N calls, parked). server 1721 (+9), integrator 329 (+5), shared 514 (+1). build/typecheck/lint + openapi/web regen green. ef52b48.
phase_outcomes.P1: Spend/concurrency budget — two NEW server-derived caps extending the existing spawn budget (canSpawn/recordSpawn/slot pool reused unchanged). (1) max concurrent ARCS: an admission gate in the systemic branch BEFORE runDriveSession (no epic created on hold) — escalateToHuman when inFlightArcs>=cap; arc count = a FRESH per-tick probe over the FULL listAcknowledgedByHolder list (pendingDrive||pendingArc && !arcComplete), GATED on autoImplementEnabled so answer-mode does zero new fetches (byte-identical). (2) max arc DURATION: a cap in advanceArc AFTER the arc_complete check (so an all-landed-this-cycle arc completes, not caps) — arcStartedAtMs reads the durable P3 pendingDrive intent-marker createdAt (fail-safe null⇒never caps); on cap → the existing arc_partial escalate (landed phases preserved on main, NO rollback — revert is P2). Both server-derived ⇒ restart-resilient. Config: autoImplement.budget{maxConcurrentArcs=100, maxArcDurationSec=604800} + 2 env vars (positiveInt); generous defaults ⇒ A1-A3 byte-identical. Noted honestly: a fixed tick-start snapshot can over-admit at maxConcurrent>1 (default 1 avoids it; cost governor not safety gate — train verify is the floor). 178 responder tests (+8). build/typecheck/lint green. Ships enabled=false. 8b8ac5e.

notes: A4 start. Depends on A2 (escalation-linked train land) + A3 (autonomous multi-phase drive — DONE @ 8460ca0). Trust-first guardrails: governance + insurance, NOT human-approval gates. P1 = spend/concurrency budget (max concurrent arcs + max arc duration) extending the existing spawn budget; exhaustion → pause+escalate. P2 = revert path (undo a landed_sha through the verify-gated train). P3 = reclaim stranded drives + submitted-but-unlanded MRs. P4 = metrics data for A5 + seal/docs/close. Ships enabled=false.
